#  Edit a Bank Account as a Payee 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##### Limited Release
[Free Procore accounts](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/free-procore-account/tutorials/create-a-free-procore-company-account) are currently available in English in Canada and the United States.
##  Background 
If you are contracted to do a job for a company using Procore Pay to manage invoice payments, you can [add a bank account](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payee-setup-guide/add-your-bank-account) to your company's account to receive payments. You can edit your bank account's nickname at any time. Additionally, if you have added multiple bank accounts, you can update the 'Deposit Bank Account' that receives payments.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)  
 _Note:_ Permissions differ based on whether your company has a free or paid Procore account. See [How can I tell if I have a free or paid Procore account?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-can-i-tell-if-i-have-a-free-or-paid-procore-account)
  * You can only designate one (1) bank account to receive payments per commitment.
  * If you have only one bank account linked, it will be set as your default bank account for invoice payments by default.
  * You can add multiple bank accounts to your Procore account.


#####  Note
If you need to edit an existing bank account to update your company's personal or financial information, you must [add it as a new bank account](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payee-setup-guide/add-your-bank-account) and [deactivate the existing bank account](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/deactivate-a-bank-account-as-a-payee).
##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to your company's homepage. See [How do I change companies?](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/login-and-account-management/tutorials/change-companies-in-procores-navigation-bar)
  2. Navigate to the **Payments** tool.
  3. Click the **Configure Settings** ![Icons Settings Gear](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8d4e4403c0eed73423d93c414c08e3a6/35b17d2e5497c876221891c1d9e5a114/icons-settings-gear.png?fm=webp&h=15) icon.
  4. Click **External Bank Accounts**.
  5. Click the **Vertical Ellipsis** ![Icon Ellipsis Vertical](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b6d0fa56ca6a66941a4032499f56aec8/2bb6315cb1db00c907bda1e2ae7a081c/icon-vertical-ellipsis.png?fm=webp&h=15) next to the bank account.
     * To update the 'Account Nickname':
       * Click **Edit**.
       * Update the 'Account Nickname'.
       * Click **Agree & Continue**.
     * To update which bank account receives payments:
       * Click **Select as Deposit Account**.


## See Also
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * [Deactivate a Bank Account as a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/deactivate-a-bank-account-as-a-payee)
title: Edit a Bank Account as a Payee
description: How to edit a bank account nickname or default account where you receive payments processed by Procore Pay.
